common_patterns.overview: Common patterns and examples for debugging DarkDelve issues using MCP validation.

pattern.name: mcp_game_initialization
pattern.description: Initialize a game instance for MCP validation
pattern.code:
from darkdelve import Game
def setup_game_for_mcp_validation(): """Initialize a fresh game instance for MCP-based debugging.""" game = Game() game.initialize() return game

pattern.name: mcp_entity_positioning
pattern.description: Position entities for specific test scenarios

pattern.name: mcp_stat_manipulation
pattern.description: Modify entity stats for edge case testing

pattern.name: mcp_inventory_setup
pattern.description: Set up inventory with specific items
pattern.code:
from darkdelve import Item, ItemType, EquipmentSlot
def add_weapon_to_inventory(entity, weapon_id, damage_bonus=5, to_hit_bonus=2): """Add a weapon to an entity's inventory and equip it.""" weapon = Item( id=weapon_id, name="Iron Sword", description="A sharp iron sword", symbol="/", item_type=ItemType.WEAPON, weight=5, value=100, damage_bonus=damage_bonus, to_hit_bonus=to_hit_bonus, equipped_slot=EquipmentSlot.MAIN_HAND ) entity.inventory.add_item(weapon) slots = entity.inventory._get_valid_slots_for_item(weapon) if slots: entity.inventory.equip(weapon.id, slots[0]) return weapon

pattern.name: mcp_action_execution
test_bug_reproduction
pattern.description: Pattern for creating tests that reproduce bugs
pattern.code:
def test_bug_reproduction_pattern(): """ Pattern for creating a test that reproduces a bug: 1. Set up the specific scenario that triggers the bug 2. Execute the action that causes the bug 3. Verify the bug manifests as expected 4. Assert the expected behavior (which should fail before fix) """ # Step 1: Set up scenario game = Game() game.initialize()
# Position entities to trigger bug game.player.x, game.player.y = 5, 5
# Step 2: Execute action game.process_action("d") # move right
# Step 3: Verify bug (this assertion will fail before fix) # assert game.player.x == expected_x, "Bug: player should not move into wall"
# Step 4: After fix, verify correct behavior # assert game.player.x == 5 # player should stay in place pass

pattern.name: test_combat_scenario
pattern.code:
from darkdelve import CombatResolver, Entity, Inventory
def test_combat_scenario_pattern(): """ Pattern for testing combat scenarios: 1. Create attacker and defender with specific stats 2. Execute combat resolution 3. Verify damage calculation 4. Verify combat event details """ # Step 1: Create entities attacker = Entity( x=0, y=0, char='@', color=(255,255,255), name="Player", blocks=True, hp=20, max_hp=20, power=10, defense=5, speed=100, inventory=Inventory(max_weight=100) )
defender = Entity( x=1, y=0, char='g', color=(100,200,100), name="Goblin", blocks=True, hp=10, max_hp=10, power=3, defense=1, speed=50, inventory=Inventory(max_weight=100) )
# Step 2: Execute combat result = CombatResolver.resolve_attack(attacker, defender)
# Step 3: Verify damage assert result.damage > 0, "Should deal damage" assert result.damage <= attacker.power + 5, "Damage should be reasonable"
# Step 4: Verify combat event assert result.event is not None, "Should have combat event" assert result.event.attacker_id == attacker.id assert result.event.defender_id == defender.id

pattern.name: test_movement_scenario
pattern.code:
def test_movement_scenario_pattern(): """ Pattern for testing movement scenarios: 1. Set up map with specific wall/floor configuration 2. Position player at specific location 3. Attempt movement in various directions 4. Verify movement is allowed/blocked as expected """ import numpy as np from darkdelve import Game
# Step 1: Create game with known map game = Game() game.initialize()
# Step 2: Position player game.player.x, game.player.y = 5, 5
# Step 3: Test movement # Check if position is walkable is_walkable = not game.dungeon_map[game.player.x + 1, game.player.y]
# Step 4: Verify movement if is_walkable: game.process_action("d") # move right assert game.player.x == 6, "Player should move right" else: game.process_action("d") # try to move right assert game.player.x == 5, "Player should not move into wall"

pattern.name: test_inventory_scenario
pattern.code:
def test_inventory_scenario_pattern(): """ Pattern for testing inventory scenarios: 1. Set up player with specific inventory state 2. Execute inventory action (pickup, drop, equip, use) 3. Verify inventory state changes 4. Verify entity state changes """ from darkdelve import Game, Item, ItemType, EquipmentSlot
# Step 1: Create game and position player game = Game() game.initialize()
# Step 2: Create and add item item = Item( id="test_sword", name="Test Sword", description="A test weapon", symbol="/", item_type=ItemType.WEAPON, weight=5, value=100, damage_bonus=5, to_hit_bonus=2, equipped_slot=EquipmentSlot.MAIN_HAND ) game.player.inventory.add_item(item)
# Step 3: Verify item added assert len(game.player.inventory.items) == 1 assert game.player.inventory.items[0].id == "test_sword"
# Step 4: Equip item slots = game.player.inventory._get_valid_slots_for_item(item) if slots: game.player.inventory.equip(item.id, slots[0]) assert game.player.inventory.equipment[slots[0]] == item
